steps.title: Identify what the example must show
steps.content: The claim: countries with very large populations may be less densely populated than countries with much smaller populations. The example needs two countries — one big-population/low-density and one small-population/high-density — for direct comparison.

steps.title: Match the data
steps.content:
(B) contrasts Angola (large pop, low density of $20.1$/km²) with São Tomé and Príncipe (smaller pop, higher density). That's exactly the contrast the claim sets up.

(A) and (D) don't compare two countries on density. (C) says the densities are similar — not the contrast claimed.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
